title: Machaerium incorruptibile
taxonomy: Machaerium incorruptibile is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the subfamily Faboideae. Further, Machaerium incorruptibile is classified as a genus and species within the Leguminosae order, which includes a vast array of plants. Its classification is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. This classification is consistent with the botanical nomenclature used in plant taxonomy.

intro: Machaerium incorruptibile, also known as the "Immortal Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Machaerium incorruptibile include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Machaerium incorruptibile is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and essential oils to treat various health conditions.
